title: Human Embryo Thawing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2eab3","text":"This protocol is a part of our collection of protocols for culturing human embryos and human stem cell-derived blastoids in an endometrial culture system.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4h110","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c22od","text":"This step can be replaced with any other method for thawing human embryos, you may wish to use your own preferred method for doing so here instead of following our protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"59hch","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3f587","text":"This protocol should be undertaken once you have assembled the endometrial scaffold and have begun the hormonal stimulation preparing the scaffold for implantation. This is performed in parallel with a portion of the \"Human Embryo or Blastoid Culture in Endometrial Scaffolds\" protocol, it is recommended that you familiarise yourself with this protocol so that you can time the thawing of your embryos appropriately to line up with when your scaffold will be ready to receive them.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: There are multiple commercially-available kits to thaw cryopreserved human embryos. Below are two kits that we have used to thaw day 5 embryos, which were then cultured in vitro with the endometrial model. i) Kitazato thawing kit (VT802-2 or VT602 , REF 91121) Catalog-EN-Vitrificaiton_2024_asia.pdf Protocol-EN-Vitrification_for_Oocytes_Embryos.pdf ii) Vit Kit Thaw by Irvine Scientific ( Fujifilm, REF 90137-SO ). Embryos were transferred between solutions using a micropipetter ( Origio , MXL3-STR-CGR ) and micropipetter flexible tips ( Origio , MXL3-275 ). The Stripper micropipettor with Comfort Grip ( micropipettor, Origio, MXL3-STR-CGR, MXL3-275 tips, https://fertility.coopersurgical.com/wp-content/uploads/Stripper_EZ-range_US_v3_web-1.pdf )
Transfer open-pulled straws containing vitrified embryos directly from liquid nitrogen into 2 mL pre-warmed thawing solution ( TS ).

After 00:01:00 immersed in TS , embryos should be completely released from the straw. Transfer embryo to a ReploPlate to continue thawing process ( ReproPlates plate, Kitazato, Hunter Scientific, 83020 )
Transfer embryos consecutively into the following solutions:

After these steps, transfer the embryo in a single drop of Embryo Culture Medium SAGE 1 step (Origio, 67010060A) supplemented with 5mg/mL Human Serum Albumin (Vitrolife, 10064), and covered by oil (Life Global, LGOL-100) Incubate for a minimum of 03:00:00 at 37 °C , 20% O 2 and 5% CO 2 to allow time for recovery after thawing prior to subsequent manipulations.
Dilution Solution ( DS , 00:03:00 for Kitazato or 00:04:00 for Irvine)
Washing Step 1 Solution ( WS1 , 00:05:00 for Kitazato or 00:04:00 for Irvine)
Washing Step 2 Solution ( WS2 , 00:01:00 for Kitazato or 00:04:00 for Irvine)
creator: Richard J Acton
